implemented: Hash-linked pause, resume, and drain transitions extend the existing default-disabled runtime control contract.
Resume requires an enabled non-stopped worker and rechecks the governed 150-GiB storage floor.
A deterministic SQLite scheduler leases pending/retry jobs by attempt then job ID under BEGIN IMMEDIATE.
The scheduler enforces the configured single-worker cap and refuses leases while disabled, paused, draining, or stopped.
Lease creation atomically updates job attempt/state and appends exact control-revision evidence.
Lease completion atomically removes the lease, records the terminal queue state, and explicitly records artifact_promoted=false.
Read-only status reports queue counts, active leases, leasing eligibility, and drained state.
JSON CLI commands implement `maskfactory daz worker status|pause|resume|drain`; mutations remain dry-run by default.
queue_behaviors_proven: default-disabled state creates no lease and leaves job/attempt/event rows unchanged
pause and drain prohibit new leases without cancelling an existing lease
drained becomes true only after the active lease finishes
two queued jobs lease deterministically and maximum_workers=1 blocks a second owner
dry-run CLI transitions do not mutate the control revision
terminal queue completion does not promote a partial or scene artifact
remaining_acceptance: Wire the scheduler to the production `maskfactory daz worker run` execution loop and exact recipe/result protocol.
Prove pause/resume/drain against the governed F-drive queue with a bounded inactive fixture plan after D1/D9 prerequisites permit it.
Prove restart/expired-lease recovery, heartbeat, resource ceilings, quiet hours and daily GPU limits in their dependent D11 items.
claim_boundary: This evidence proves inactive queue/control implementation and deterministic fixture behavior only. It does not activate DAZ, run a renderer, mutate the live queue, prove the production worker loop, complete MF-P9-12.01, or satisfy the seven-day soak/recovery gates.
